marketState: bearish-leaning: oversupplied medium-term, short-term caught between oversold bounce and bearish trend
summary: Front-month NG is trading essentially at fair value ($2.906 vs $2.917 fair, spread -$0.011) but sits well below its seasonal median (-11.3pp, -14.7% YTD vs -3.4% median), with below-50% odds of higher prices at 1/2/4 weeks (38%/38%/31%) and a typical -3.1% 4-week drift into the cooling season, though seasonality reliability is low (50pp spread). Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise, and managed money is net short (mmNet -60,295, 35th percentile) - both consistent with an oversupplied, softly bearish backdrop. Near-term weather demand is running hot (+12.5% vs 5y, rangePos 0.75, rising +22% vs normal), which offers short-term counter-support and likely explains the deeply oversold 4H RSI (23) and price sitting below the 1D lower Bollinger band even as all six technical timeframes remain in a bearish 50<200 EMA trend. Net take: the medium-term bias favors fading rallies, while the short term is too conflicted (hot weather/oversold vs bearish trend) to chase in either direction right now.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; scale ~half off at TP1, trail remainder to TP2
longTerm.rationale: Storage sits +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf weekly surprise, seasonality shows sub-50% odds of higher prices at 1/2/4 weeks with a typical -3.1% 4-week move into the cooling season, and managed money is already net short (35th percentile) reinforcing the bearish skew. Fair value is essentially in line with spot (no overvaluation to fight), so short entries near current levels do not require chasing.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained heat wave that pulls storage deviation back toward the 5y average, or price closing back above the plus21D fair-value band midpoint (~$3.0) with managed-money short-covering, would invalidate this thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Signals conflict: near-term weather demand is hot and rising (+12.5% vs 5y, rangePos 0.75) and 4H RSI is deeply oversold (23) with price below the 1D lower Bollinger band, both arguing for a bounce, yet all six technical timeframes still show a bearish 50<200 EMA trend and negative MACD on 4H/1D/1W. Price is fairly valued at every horizon, so there is no mispricing edge to trade either direction right now.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a bounce into the $2.95-$3.05 fair-value zone to sell for a trend-continuation short, or a confirmed break below ~$2.78 (near the 1D lower Bollinger band) to join the downtrend; avoid chasing at the current oversold extreme.

keyDrivers: Storage +184 Bcf above 5-year average with a +72 Bcf bearish weekly surprise
Seasonality: sub-50% odds of higher prices at 1/2/4 weeks (38%/38%/31%), typical -3.1% 4-week drift, but reliability is low
All six technical timeframes bearish (50<200 EMA), yet 4H RSI (23) and 1D sub-lower-Bollinger reading are oversold
Near-term weather demand +12.5% vs 5y and rising (+22% vs normal), providing short-term support
Price is essentially at fair value across all horizons ($2.906 vs $2.917 now), no major mispricing edge
risks: Low seasonality reliability (50pp 10-90% spread) means the seasonal edge could easily fail to play out
A sustained heat wave could accelerate power-burn demand and shrink the storage surplus faster than expected
